skill.name: Azure.Search.Documents (.NET)
skill.description: Azure AI Search SDK for .NET. Use for building search applications with full-text, vector, semantic, and hybrid search capabilities. Covers SearchClient for queries and document CRUD, SearchIndexClient for index management, and SearchIndexerClient for indexers and skillsets.
skill.summary: Build search applications with Azure AI Search using .NET - supports full-text, vector, semantic, and hybrid search
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This is a documentation-only skill containing SKILL.md with code examples. No executable code is present. Static analysis found 0 files scanned, 0 potential security issues, and a risk score of 0/100. The skill provides informational content about Azure Search SDK usage without any network, filesystem, or command execution capabilities.

content.user_title: Build Azure AI Search Apps with .NET
content.value_statement: Developers need to integrate powerful search capabilities into .NET applications but face complexity with the Azure Search SDK. This skill provides code examples and best practices for implementing full-text, vector, semantic, and hybrid search with the Azure.Search.Documents package.
actual_capabilities: Generate code for SearchClient to query indexes and perform document CRUD operations
Create and manage search indexes using SearchIndexClient with FieldBuilder or manual field definitions
Implement vector search with VectorizedQuery and HnswAlgorithmConfiguration
Configure semantic search with captions, answers, and semantic ranking
Build hybrid search combining keyword, vector, and semantic search
Handle authentication using DefaultAzureCredential or AzureKeyCredential
limitations: Does not execute code or make actual Azure API calls - provides only code examples
Does not create or manage Azure resources directly
Requires existing Azure AI Search service and index to be functional
Does not provide runtime debugging or troubleshooting of live implementations

use_cases.title: Full-Text Search Implementation
use_cases.description: Add keyword search to .NET applications with filtering, sorting, faceting, and pagination using SearchClient and SearchOptions.
use_cases.target_user: .NET developers building e-commerce or content search features

use_cases.title: Vector Search Integration
use_cases.description: Implement semantic vector search in .NET apps using embeddings and the VectorizedQuery API for AI-powered relevance.
use_cases.target_user: ML engineers adding semantic search to applications

use_cases.title: Hybrid Search Application
use_cases.description: Combine full-text, vector, and semantic search for maximum relevance in .NET applications.
use_cases.target_user: Full-stack developers building enterprise search solutions

prompt_templates.title: Basic Search Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to set up a SearchClient with DefaultAzureCredential and perform a basic search query with filters in a .NET application using Azure.Search.Documents.
prompt_templates.scenario: Getting started with Azure Search in .NET

prompt_templates.title: Create Search Index
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate code to create a search index with the FieldBuilder using model attributes for a Hotel class with key, filterable, sortable, and searchable fields in C#.

prompt_templates.title: Vector Search Query
prompt_templates.prompt: Write C# code to perform a vector search using VectorizedQuery with HNSW algorithm configuration and return results with their similarity scores.

prompt_templates.title: Semantic Search with Answers
prompt_templates.prompt: Show how to configure semantic search with QueryCaption and QueryAnswer to get extracted answers and highlighted captions from Azure AI Search.

output_examples.input: How do I upload documents to an Azure Search index?
output_examples.output: Use SearchClient.UploadDocumentsAsync() for new documents, MergeDocumentsAsync() for updates, or MergeOrUploadDocumentsAsync() for upsert operations. Create an IndexDocumentsBatch for multiple operations.

DefaultAzureCredential (recommended for production) uses managed identity, service principal, or VS credentials. AzureKeyCredential is available but not recommended for production due to key management concerns.

best_practices: Use DefaultAzureCredential over API keys for production environments to leverage managed identity
Use FieldBuilder with model attributes for type-safe index definitions and automatic schema generation
Batch document operations using IndexDocumentsBatch for better throughput and reduced API calls
anti_patterns: Using API keys in production code instead of DefaultAzureCredential with managed identity
Loading all fields with Select when only specific fields are needed
Not handling RequestFailedException for proper error handling and debugging

faq.question: What is the difference between SearchClient, SearchIndexClient, and SearchIndexerClient?
faq.answer: SearchClient is used for querying indexes and CRUD operations on documents. SearchIndexClient creates and manages indexes and synonym maps. SearchIndexerClient manages indexers, skillsets, and data sources.

faq.question: How do I handle authentication in development vs production?
faq.answer: Use DefaultAzureCredential which automatically selects the best authentication method. In development, it uses Visual Studio credentials. In production on Azure, it uses Managed Identity.

faq.question: Can I use this skill to create an Azure Search service?
faq.answer: No, this skill provides code examples for the SDK. You need to create the Azure AI Search service through Azure Portal, CLI, or ARM templates separately.

faq.question: What is hybrid search?
faq.answer: Hybrid search combines keyword search, vector search, and semantic ranking to provide the most relevant results. It uses Reciprocal Rank Fusion to combine results from different search approaches.

faq.question: How do I add vector search to my index?
faq.answer: Add a field with VectorSearchField attribute, configure VectorSearchProfile with HnswAlgorithmConfiguration, and use VectorizedQuery to search with embeddings.

faq.question: What are the key differences between v11 and earlier versions?
faq.answer: Version 11 introduced improved async patterns, better error handling with RequestFailedException, and updated field builder syntax. Earlier versions used different client initialization patterns.
